subject: Computer Networking
title: Patch Panels and Racks
description: A 60-second lesson on Patch Panels and Racks.

hook.text: Behind every organized server room is a patch panel: a tidy grid of ports connecting cables to gear.

buildup.text: Cables from wall outlets terminate at the panel. Short patch cables then connect the panel to switches.

discovery.text: You never unplug permanent cables. Changes happen at the panel with easy-to-swap short cords.

twist.text: Standard 19-inch racks hold switches, servers, and panels. One U equals 1.75 inches of height.

climax.text: Good cable management prevents airflow blockage and simplifies troubleshooting. Engineering meets art.

punchline.text: Patch panels bring order to chaos. Organized cables save hours.

quiz.question: Why do network engineers use patch panels?
options: To make cable changes easy without disturbing permanent wiring
